name: Dehesa de Abajo Circular Trail
name_original: Laguna Dehesa de Abajo — circular por la Reserva Natural Concertada Dehesa de Abajo
description: Jog this 7.1-mile (11.3 km) moderate circular route through the Natural Reserve Dehesa de Abajo, enjoying diverse wildlife and scenery.
region_intro: Jogging routes La Puebla del Río are set within a landscape shaped by the Guadalquivir River, extensive marshlands, and agricultural areas. The region, bordering Doñana National Park, features flat plains, rice fields, and pine forests, providing varied terrain for runners. These natural environments offer numerous paths and tracks, making it suitable for both short and long-distance running. The area's low elevation changes contribute to generally accessible routes. Best jogging routes around La Puebla Del Río The most popular jogging route is Dehesa de Abajo Circular Trail, a 7.1 miles (11.4 km) trail that takes 1 hour 16 minutes to complete. This route explores the diverse ecosystems of marshland and pine forest within a natural reserve. Another top favourite among local runners is Cañada de Arrayanes – Eucalyptus Trail loop from La Puebla del Río, a difficult 18.9 miles (30.4 km) path. This longer route features varied terrain, including eucalyptus trails and open landscapes, with an elevation gain of over 260 meters. Local runners also love the Statue of Hasekura Tsunenaga loop from La Puebla del Río, a 4.0 miles (6.4 km) trail leading through riverside areas, often completed in about 44 minutes. Jogging around La Puebla Del Río is defined by riverine paths, marshlands, and rice fields. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy loops to more challenging long-distance runs. The routes in La Puebla Del Río are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 3.9 stars from over 15 reviews. More than 900 runners have used komoot to explore La Puebla Del Río's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many running routes are available in La Puebla del Río?
A: There are over 70 dedicated running routes around La Puebla del Río, offering a wide variety of options for different skill levels and preferences. The komoot community has explored these trails over 900 times.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on the jogging routes in La Puebla del Río?
A: The terrain in La Puebla del Río is largely defined by its natural surroundings. You'll find flat plains, scenic riverside paths along the Guadalquivir, extensive marshlands, and routes winding through rice fields and pine forests. The area generally features low elevation changes, making many routes accessible.

Q: Are there any easy running trails suitable for beginners in La Puebla del Río?
A: Yes, there are several easy running routes perfect for beginners. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31600251" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Running loop from La Puebla del Río&lt;/a&gt; is an easy 4.3 km path that offers a gentle introduction to the area's natural beauty.

Q: Can I find long-distance running trails in La Puebla del Río?
A: Absolutely. For those looking for a longer challenge, routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/17262356" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cañada de Arrayanes – Eucalyptus Trail loop from La Puebla del Río&lt;/a&gt; offer a difficult 30.4 km path with varied terrain and significant elevation gain, ideal for endurance training.

Q: What are some interesting natural features or landmarks I might see while running in La Puebla del Río?
A: While running, you can explore diverse landscapes including the extensive marshlands, the Guadalquivir River, and vast rice fields. You might also encounter protected areas like the Dehesa de Abajo Natural Reserve, known for its birdlife and pine forests. Highlights like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5324565" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Parque Periurbano de La Corchuela&lt;/a&gt; offer beautiful forest settings, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3987374" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Guadaíra Riverside Walk&lt;/a&gt; provides scenic river views.

Q: Are there any circular running routes in the area?
A: Yes, many routes in La Puebla del Río are circular, allowing you to start and finish in the same location. A popular option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1014200676/dehesa-de-abajo-circular-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Dehesa de Abajo Circular Trail&lt;/a&gt;, which is a moderate 11.3 km loop exploring marshland and pine forest ecosystems.

Q: What do other runners say about the trails in La Puebla del Río?
A: The running routes in La Puebla del Río are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 3.9 stars from over 15 reviews. Runners often praise the diverse natural environments, from tranquil riverside paths to the unique experience of running through rice fields and pine forests.

Q: Are there any trails that pass by lakes or offer water views?
A: Yes, the region's proximity to marshlands and natural reserves means you can find routes with water views. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1014200676/dehesa-de-abajo-circular-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Dehesa de Abajo Circular Trail&lt;/a&gt;, for instance, explores floodable marshland. You can also find viewpoints like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5379309" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Laguna Dehesa de Abajo Viewpoint&lt;/a&gt;, offering scenic vistas of the lagoons and their birdlife.

Q: What is the best time of year to go jogging in La Puebla del Río?
A: Given its location in southern Spain, spring and autumn generally offer the most pleasant temperatures for jogging in La Puebla del Río. These seasons provide comfortable conditions to enjoy the diverse landscapes, including the riverbanks, marshlands, and pine forests, without the intense heat of summer.

Q: Are there any running routes near the Guadalquivir River?
A: Yes, the Guadalquivir River runs through the municipal area, offering numerous scenic riverside routes. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/21851237" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Statue of Hasekura Tsunenaga loop from La Puebla del Río&lt;/a&gt; is a great example, leading through riverside areas and providing beautiful views of the river.

Q: Are there any specific trails that highlight the region's unique rice fields?
A: Yes, the extensive rice fields are a significant feature of the landscape. While specific named routes through the rice fields are not listed, many local paths and tracks, often referred to as 'rutas por arrozales', traverse these areas, offering a distinctive and flat jogging experience, especially around the Guadalquivir River.

Q: What kind of natural reserves can I explore while running in La Puebla del Río?
A: La Puebla del Río is a gateway to several important natural reserves. You can explore the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1014200676/dehesa-de-abajo-circular-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Dehesa de Abajo Natural Reserve&lt;/a&gt;, known for its marshland, olive groves, and pine forests. The region is also close to the Brazo del Este Natural Park and the Cañada de los Pájaros, all offering rich ecosystems and potential running paths.
tips: This is the visitor center for this area called Dehesa de Abajo. There is information and a bar/restaurant to make a stop after or before the walk!!
Lookout from where you can observe the lagoon, where depending on the year if you can find more or less water. Perfect space to see many birds in a quiet and green space!

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to jog the Laguna Dehesa de Abajo circular route?
faqs.answer: The route is designed for jogging and typically takes around 1 hour and 15 minutes to complete. This estimate is based on the trail's distance and average jogging pace.

faqs.question: What is the difficulty level of this jogging route?
faqs.answer: This route is rated as moderate for jogging. While it requires a reasonable level of fitness, the paths are mostly accessible, and the elevation gain is minimal (around 46 meters), making it a good option for those looking for a moderately challenging but not overly strenuous run.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain will I encounter on the trail?
faqs.answer: You'll experience a diverse mix of Mediterranean forest and marshland habitats. The paths are primarily unpaved, natural, and gravel, with very few paved sections. Expect to jog through spectacular pine forests, areas with wild olive trees, and scrubland.

faqs.question: What notable features or points of interest are along the route?
faqs.answer: The trail offers exceptional natural beauty and abundant wildlife. You can visit the Dehesa de Abajo Visitor Center (Puebla del Río), which is a convenient starting point. Don't miss the Laguna Dehesa de Abajo Viewpoint for stunning views and excellent birdwatching opportunities.

faqs.question: Is the Laguna Dehesa de Abajo circular route dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: While the Natural Reserve Dehesa de Abajo generally welcomes visitors, it's important to check the specific regulations regarding dogs within the protected area. Often, dogs are permitted but must be kept on a leash to protect the abundant wildlife, especially during nesting seasons. Please confirm local signage or with the visitor center upon arrival.

faqs.question: Do I need a permit or pay an entrance fee to access the Reserva Natural Concertada Dehesa de Abajo?
faqs.answer: Generally, there is no entrance fee to access the Reserva Natural Concertada Dehesa de Abajo or its trails. However, it's always a good idea to check with the Dehesa de Abajo Visitor Center for any specific regulations or temporary restrictions that might be in place.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to jog this trail?
faqs.answer: The best times to visit are typically spring (March to May) and autumn (September to November). During these seasons, the weather is milder, making for more comfortable jogging conditions, and wildlife activity, especially birdwatching, is often at its peak. Summers can be very hot, and winters can be cooler and wetter.

faqs.question: What wildlife can I expect to see while jogging?
faqs.answer: This reserve is a significant birdwatching destination. From observatories, you might spot flamingos, spoonbills, red-crested pochards, mallards, and endangered species like the red-knobbed coot and marbled teal. Raptors such as black kites and booted eagles can be seen overhead, and the wild olive groves host Spain's largest colony of white storks. More elusive animals like foxes and badgers also inhabit the area.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access the Laguna Dehesa de Abajo circular trail?
faqs.answer: You can typically find parking near the Dehesa de Abajo Visitor Center (Puebla del Río), which serves as a convenient starting point for the trail. The visitor center also offers amenities like information and a bar/restaurant.

faqs.question: Does this trail intersect with any other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route also passes through sections of the Puebla del Río-Aznalcazar trail and the Transandalus trail.

faqs.question: Is the trail suitable for families with children?
faqs.answer: While rated moderate for jogging, the trail's minimal elevation and mostly accessible paths make it suitable for families with children who are used to walking or can manage a longer distance. The diverse wildlife and natural beauty offer plenty of engagement for younger visitors.

waypoints.name: Dehesa de Abajo Visitor Center (Puebla del Río)
